findings.evidence: Lines 49-61 show `@[expose] def stdComplexLineEnergyDensity ...` immediately followed by `lemma stdComplexLineEnergyDensity_def ... := rfl`.
findings.fix: Remove `@[expose]` from the definition and use the defining lemma as the public characterization.
findings.issue: `stdComplexLineEnergyDensity` is marked `@[expose]`, so downstream users can unfold the implementation body even though the file already provides `stdComplexLineEnergyDensity_def`; this over-exposes the surface for a bookkeeping definition.

findings.evidence: Lines 56-61 define `stdComplexLineEnergyDensity_def`; nearby analogous apply/def normal forms such as `associatedBilinForm_apply` are tagged `@[simp]` in `TauCeti/Geometry/Symplectic/AlmostComplex.lean:318`.
findings.fix: Add `@[simp]` to `stdComplexLineEnergyDensity_def` after removing the exposed body.
findings.issue: The defining normal-form lemma for the new density is not tagged `@[simp]`, so `simp` will not normalize the public density to the associated-bilinear-form sum.

summary: The new energy-density API exposes the body directly and does not make its defining normal form available to automation. Keep the body hidden and expose the formula through the characteristic lemma instead.
